title: For the Prodigal's Parent
scripture_text: While he was still a long way off, his father saw him and was moved with compassion, and ran and embraced him and kissed him.
reflection: They may not come back. But you can keep the door open. That is the whole of your work, the whole of your hope.
body:
You are waiting. You have been waiting for years, for a call, for a sign, for a turn. The waiting is its own kind of loss, and it is real, and it is allowed to hurt.

You are not a failure. You did not cause this. You cannot control it. The Lord knows this. The Lord weeps with you. The parable of the lost son is, more often than not, told to the parent who is left behind.

The father in the story did not go find the son. He waited. He kept the door. He watched the road. He was ready when the son, finally, came over the hill.

You do not know if your child will come over the hill. But you can be ready. You can keep the door. The Lord is with you in the watching.
prayer: Lord, I am waiting. Be patient with me. And bring them home. In Your time. Amen.
